name: Mao Feng
region: Anhui, China (generic style also produced in Yunnan, Sichuan, Zhejiang)
terroir: Varies by production region — best examples from high-altitude misty mountains
flavor_profile: Mao Feng is a broad style of Chinese green tea characterized by one bud and one or two leaves with silvery fuzz ('fur') and a peak-like shape. The general profile is smooth and mellow with gentle floral notes, soft grassiness, and a clean finish. Nuttiness and lingering sweetness are common. Huangshan Mao Feng from Anhui is the most famous origin, but Yunnan and Mengding versions offer their own regional character.

brewing.tips: A general style — parameters suit most Mao Feng origins. Adjust temperature down to 80°C for very tender spring pickings.
